name: MS Portable Office Cabin
description:
A standard portable office cabin handles a two-year deployment at a commercial premises without difficulty. Put the same cabin in an industrial environment — a manufacturing campus in Hosur, a processing plant near Mangalore, an admin block at a factory in Tumkur — and the frame joints begin to show corrosion before year three. An MS portable office cabin is built to a different structural standard: IS 2062 Grade A mild steel throughout the frame, the same steel grade used in industrial buildings and factory structures. This page covers that specification, who it is the right choice for, and what it costs. If you are still comparing options across the full range, start with our portable office range first.

What MS Grade Means in a Portable Office Frame

Mild steel is a broad term. IS 2062 Grade A is a specific standard — India's structural steel specification, with a minimum yield strength of 250 MPa and verified weldability at all section sizes. These properties matter in a cabin frame because a portable office is not static in the way a brick building is. It is loaded and unloaded, sits on surfaces that shift with temperature, and in South India goes through 40 or more thermal cycles a year between the hottest and coolest months. A frame built to IS 2062 holds its weld integrity and dimensional accuracy across those cycles without progressive joint loosening.

IS 2062 Grade A — what the standard requires for a long-duration cabin frame

The main structural members in an MS portable office cabin frame are C-section mild steel channels: 100 × 50 mm for primary frame members, 75 × 40 mm for cross members. These are factory-welded at every junction — not bolted. Bolted joints loosen under vibration and thermal cycling. Properly executed factory welds on IS 2062 members do not.

Surface coating on every MS cabin frame: two-coat epoxy zinc phosphate primer applied directly to bare steel, followed by synthetic enamel topcoat. This coating system, applied before panel installation, protects the weld surfaces for 8–12 years under normal industrial conditions before recoating is needed.

How the MS frame handles conditions a standard cabin struggles with

Standard portable office cabins use thinner-section steel frames. For a startup office at a commercial park in Bangalore, this specification is fully adequate. For an admin office on a manufacturing campus near a coast, or next to a production line that runs heavy machinery continuously, a thinner-section frame begins to deform at cross-member welds within three to four years. Salt-laden air, vibration, and humidity cycles accelerate this. An IS 2062 Grade A frame with heavier C-section members handles all three of these conditions without structural maintenance beyond periodic surface inspection.

Where an MS Portable Office Cabin Is the Right Call

MS portable office cabins deployed at a manufacturing campus in South India. IS 2062 Grade A frame, factory-fitted interiors, ready for long-duration plant admin use.

The buyers who specify MS grade run workspaces that stay in place for at least three years, usually in environments harder on materials than a standard commercial office premises.

Manufacturing and industrial campuses. A factory in electronics, pharma, auto components, or textiles needs on-site HR, operations, and admin offices that function for the life of the plant — often 10 to 20 years. Replacing office cabins every four years because the standard-spec frame has deteriorated is disruptive and uneconomical. An MS cabin specified once stays structurally sound for the full plant cycle with basic surface maintenance.

Coastal and high-humidity deployments. Karnataka's coastal belt, Tamil Nadu's coastal districts, and Andhra Pradesh's port towns accelerate corrosion in thinner-gauge frames. The IS 2062 frame combined with 60 mm PUF panels — a specified upgrade — and marine-grade enamel finish gives a coastal MS cabin a service life comparable to an inland standard-spec cabin.

Sites with limited cabin-replacement access. A remote processing facility, a hill station utility plant, or a wind farm site where getting a replacement cabin delivered costs more than the premium of specifying correctly the first time.

If the deployment is 12–24 months at a standard commercial premises in Bangalore, Hyderabad, or Chennai, a standard-spec portable office cabin is the more cost-appropriate choice. See the full portable office cabin range for those options.

Structural and Interior Specifications

Frame, wall panels, and roof assembly

Component Specification
Main frame members IS 2062 Grade A mild steel, C-section 100 × 50 mm
Cross members IS 2062 Grade A mild steel, C-section 75 × 40 mm
Frame connection Factory-welded at all junctions
Wall panels 40 mm PUF sandwich panel, PPGI outer face
Roof sheets PPGI 0.47 mm TCT corrugated, PU foam underlay
Floor substrate 18 mm BWP-grade plywood on MS frame
Floor finish 1.8 mm vinyl, laid wall-to-wall
Surface coating 2-coat: epoxy zinc phosphate primer + synthetic enamel topcoat

Factory fabrication of an MS portable office cabin frame at SAMAN's Bangalore facility. IS 2062 Grade A C-section members, factory-welded at all junctions, with 40mm PUF sandwich panel being fitted.

Coastal upgrade available: 60 mm PUF panels + marine-grade enamel — specify at order stage.

Standard interior office fittings

Every MS portable office cabin is fully fitted inside the Bangalore factory before dispatch:

  • False ceiling with integrated LED light panels
  • 1 AC point — bracket, 15A outlet, wired to MCB — buyer fits split unit on delivery day
  • 1 steel-stud internal partition at buyer-specified position
  • 2 aluminium sliding windows with MS grills
  • 1 lockable MS flush door, weathersealed, with latch and deadbolt
  • MCB distribution board: 4 × 5A sockets, 1 × 15A socket, earthed and tested before dispatch

Additional partitions, extra AC points, upgraded flooring, and toilet or pantry attachments are available. All go into the factory order, not a site visit. Confirm at quote stage so fabrication includes them.

Available Sizes and Floor Area

Cabin size Internal area Seated capacity Approx. MS frame weight
10 × 10 ft 100 sq ft 2–3 people ~1.4 tonnes
10 × 12 ft 120 sq ft 3 people ~1.6 tonnes
10 × 14 ft 140 sq ft 3–4 people ~1.8 tonnes
10 × 20 ft 200 sq ft 5–6 people ~2.8 tonnes
12 × 20 ft 240 sq ft 6–8 people ~3.2 tonnes
14 × 24 ft 336 sq ft 8–10 people ~4.1 tonnes

MS portable office cabins from SAMAN's standard size range — 10×10 ft (2–3 people), 10×14 ft (3–4 people), and 10×20 ft (5–6 people). All fabricated to order in Bangalore.

All sizes are fabricated to order. A 5-tonne forklift with extended forks, or a crane, handles placement at all sizes. Confirm gate width and ground-bearing capacity at the destination site before confirming the order.

MS Grade or Standard Frame — Which Portable Office Cabin Do You Need?

This is the question that comes up in nearly every MS cabin enquiry. The short answer: MS grade earns its premium when the deployment is long, the environment is demanding, or both. For short deployments at standard commercial sites, a standard-spec cabin delivers the same workspace at a lower unit cost.

Decision factor MS portable office cabin Standard portable office cabin
Deployment length 3 years and above 1–3 years
Right environment Industrial plant, coastal, high-humidity, vibration-exposed Commercial office park, institutional campus, standard urban premises
Relocation frequency Static or low-frequency (once every 2–3 years) Moderate to frequent moves
Starting price From Rs 3,80,000 From Rs 3,20,000
Frame recoat interval 8–12 years 4–6 years

MS portable office cabin deployed long-term at an industrial manufacturing compound. Right: standard portable office cabin at a commercial business premises. Different specifications for different deployment needs.

If your site and timeline sit in the right column, browse the full range of standard portable office cabin options. If they sit in the left column, the specifications and pricing below are for your build.

What an MS Portable Office Cabin Costs

A 10 × 12 ft MS portable office cabin, fully fitted as specified above, starts at Rs 3,80,000. This covers the IS 2062 factory-welded frame, 40 mm PUF wall panels and PPGI roof, false ceiling, vinyl flooring, AC point, MCB board and outlets, one internal partition at your specified position, two aluminium windows, one lockable MS door, and delivery with placement to Bangalore, Hyderabad, Chennai, or the Greater Noida zone.

Variation Approximate addition
10 × 14 ft size + Rs 30,000–45,000
10 × 20 ft size + Rs 80,000–1,10,000
Coastal spec (60 mm PUF + marine enamel) + Rs 25,000–40,000
Additional internal partition + Rs 15,000–22,000
Additional AC point + Rs 8,000–12,000
Attached toilet provision + Rs 35,000–55,000
Outstation delivery beyond 300 km Quoted on pincode

Payment: 50% advance to start fabrication, 50% on delivery day.

Preparing Your Site Before Delivery

An MS portable office cabin being placed by crane at an industrial site. The IS 2062 frame base channels carry the lifting load. Ground workers guide from a safe distance.

An MS portable office cabin is 15–20% heavier than a standard-spec cabin of the same floor area, because the IS 2062 frame uses heavier C-section members. Confirm three things before the delivery date:

A level surface. Flat within ±20 mm across the full cabin footprint. A concrete pad, compacted earth, or existing floor slab works. A sloped or soft surface needs levelling before the unit arrives.

Crane or forklift access. All MS cabin sizes above 10 × 10 ft need a 5-tonne forklift minimum, or a crane, for placement. Gate width: 12 ft minimum for a 10 ft wide cabin; 14 ft for larger sizes. Overhead clearance: 4.5 metres minimum for a forklift lift. Confirm your site's gate dimensions and ground-bearing capacity before dispatch.

A 230V power point within 10 metres. The MCB board inside the cabin connects to this on delivery day. The cabin is powered on and ready the same day it is placed.

Transit from Bangalore: 1–3 days to South India destinations. Northern locations quoted separately on pincode.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much heavier is an MS portable office cabin than a standard cabin?

The IS 2062 frame uses heavier-section C-channels, which adds 15–20% to the total cabin weight. A 10 × 20 ft MS unit weighs approximately 2.8–3.2 tonnes versus 2.3–2.6 tonnes for a standard-spec unit of the same size. The delivery method is the same — crane or 5-tonne forklift — but confirm the lifting capacity of the equipment at your destination site before dispatch.

How long does the IS 2062 frame last before structural maintenance is needed?

With factory-applied two-coat epoxy and enamel finish, the frame at weld joints needs recoating at 8–12 years under normal industrial conditions. This is a surface task, not a structural repair. A standard-spec frame with thinner-gauge steel typically reaches the same maintenance point at 4–6 years. For buyers planning 8–10 year deployments, this difference is the central economic argument for MS grade.

Can an MS portable office cabin be used at a coastal site in South India?

Yes, with the right specification. Sites within 50 km of the coast need 60 mm PUF panels — better vapour barrier than 40 mm — and marine-grade enamel topcoat instead of standard synthetic enamel. Specify this at order stage. It adds Rs 25,000–40,000 to the unit price and significantly extends surface life in salt-air conditions.

Does the MS premium make sense for a 2-year deployment?

Not usually. For a 1–3 year deployment at a standard commercial premises, a standard-spec portable office cabin delivers the same workspace at Rs 60,000–80,000 less. The MS premium earns back through reduced surface maintenance over a 5-plus year deployment. If you are unsure which specification suits your timeline and environment, share the details in your enquiry and we will recommend accordingly.

What happens if the MS cabin needs to be relocated?

The IS 2062 frame handles crane lifts and flatbed transit without structural concern. For infrequent relocation — once every two or three years — the MS cabin moves without any special preparation beyond standard crane and level-surface requirements. For frequent relocation on a quarterly or monthly pattern, the MS portable office cabin is not optimized for that use. See the prefab mobile office specification for repeat-relocation builds.

Can I add an attached toilet or pantry to an MS portable office cabin?

Yes. A toilet attachment adds a plumbing circuit, floor drain, waterproofed panel junction, and ventilation outlet. A pantry adds a sink, drain, and counter provision. Both are incorporated into the factory build before dispatch — not fitted on site. Confirm at enquiry stage so fabrication includes them.

Is IS 2062 Grade A the same steel standard used in industrial buildings?

Yes. IS 2062 Grade A is India's structural steel specification, used in industrial sheds, factory buildings, and steel-frame structures. In a cabin, the section sizes are smaller because the load spans are smaller — but the steel grade, weld quality requirements, and corrosion protection principles are the same.

Get a Quote for Your MS Portable Office Cabin

Share four things: site location (city or district), floor area needed, type of environment (manufacturing plant, outdoor industrial, coastal, or standard commercial), and how long the cabin needs to stay in place. We will confirm whether MS grade or a different specification is the right call and send a complete priced quotation within 24 hours.

Fabrication and delivery: 8–15 working days from order confirmation, depending on size and specification. Factory open Monday to Saturday, 10 am to 5 pm, Bangalore.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937
short_description:
SAMAN Portable · Bangalore Factory-Built
MS Portable Office Cabin · IS 2062 Grade A Frame

An MS portable office cabin is built on an IS 2062 Grade A mild steel frame — factory-fitted with false ceiling, AC point, vinyl flooring, and MCB board. Right for industrial sites and deployments of 3 years or more. Starting from Rs 3,80,000 for a 10×12 ft unit, delivered and placed at your site in 8–15 working days.

Size
10×10 to 14×24 ft
Capacity
2 to 10 people
Frame
IS 2062 Grade A MS
Panel
40mm PUF Sandwich
Price
From Rs 3,80,000
Delivery
8–15 working days
Weight: 1.4–4.1 T by size · Static / infrequent relocation
SAMAN Portable
